title: The best time to visit Vietnam, region by region
excerpt: When the north is misty and the south is golden — how to time your trip for the weather you want.
content:
## North, Central & South

Vietnam spans many microclimates. The north has a real winter; the centre is best in spring; the south is warm year-round. Plan around the season you want, not a single "best" month.

### The north

March to May and September to November are the sweet spots.

title: Two unhurried days in Hội An
excerpt: Lanterns, tailors, and riverside mornings — a slow itinerary for the old town.
content:
## Day 1

Wander the old town before the crowds, then a tailor fitting and a lantern-lit dinner.

## Day 2

Cycle to the rice paddies and the beach, returning for the night market.

title: A morning at the Mekong floating markets
excerpt: Dawn on the delta: what to expect, what to eat, and how to find the quieter channels.
content:
## Start before sunrise

The markets are busiest at dawn. Hire a small boat to slip into the narrow channels the big tours miss.

title: Packing for a Sa Pa trek: a short, honest list
excerpt: Boots, layers, and the three things everyone forgets before a mountain homestay.
content:
## The essentials

Broken-in boots, a rain layer, and a head torch. Homestays provide bedding, so travel light.

## What people forget

A dry bag, blister plasters, and cash for the villages.

title: A short guide to Vietnamese coffee
excerpt: From cà phê sữa đá to egg coffee — what to order and where it comes from.
content:
## The basics

Strong robusta, slow drip, sweetened condensed milk. Egg coffee is a Hà Nội invention worth seeking out.

title: Is the Hà Giang loop too dangerous for beginners?
excerpt: An honest look at the risks of Vietnam’s most famous motorbike route — and how to ride it safely.
content:
## The honest answer

The roads are mountainous and the weather turns fast. If you have never ridden, take an easy-rider rather than a rental.

title: A first-timer’s week in northern Vietnam
excerpt: Hà Nội, Hạ Long and Ninh Bình in seven days without rushing — a suggested shape for a first trip.
content:
## The shape of the week

Two nights in Hà Nội, an overnight cruise, then the karsts of Ninh Bình before flying south.

title: Eating your way through the Hà Nội Old Quarter
excerpt: Eight dishes, eight streets — a hungry walk through the heart of the capital.
content:
## Where to start

Begin with phở for breakfast, then bún chả for lunch, and finish the day with egg coffee.

title: The caves of Phong Nha, explained
excerpt: From day-trip grottoes to the world’s largest cave — which one is right for you?
content:
## The options

Paradise Cave for grandeur on a day trip, Sơn Đoòng for a once-in-a-lifetime expedition.

title: A Complete and Thoroughly Considered Field Guide to Choosing Between the Overnight Cruises, Day Trips, and Multi-Day Packages That Sail the Waters of Hạ Long an
excerpt: Everything you need to weigh up before you book a cruise on Hạ Long or Lan Hạ Bay, from cabin classes and crowd levels to the difference a third day makes and how to avoid the busiest routes, distilled into one practical and readable guide for first-time visitors to the bay. Everything you need to w
content:
## Read this first

The bay is bigger and busier than most people expect. This guide walks through every option in plain terms.
